songs.title: Almost Called
songs.description: 2 AM. Phone in hand. Thumb hovering over the name you swore you'd delete. You almost called — and that almost is the whole story. This song is for everyone who's been one tap away from undoing all the healing they fought for.
songs.seo_description: That 2 AM moment when your thumb hovers over their name and your healing hangs by a thread. Almost Called is an original country ballad by The Dogmother for anyone who's been one tap away from undoing everything they fought for.

songs.title: Beast Mode JT
songs.description: JT goes full throttle. This is the song that plays when you stop asking for permission and start taking what's yours. High energy, no apologies, beast mode activated.

songs.title: Bigfoot Lives Here
songs.description: When you live on a ranch with Leonbergers — 170-pound dogs that look like bears — you learn that home isn't a place. It's a feeling. This is the Bigfoot Ranch anthem. The dogs wrote it. I just held the pen.
songs.seo_description: When your 170-pound Leonberger steals your La-Z-Boy and your heart in the same afternoon. Bigfoot Lives Here is the original ranch anthem by The Dogmother -- a love letter to the giant dogs who run the house.
songs.audio_path: Bigfoot Lives Here _ Bigfoot Leonberger Ranch Anthem.mp3
songs.lyrics:
He hauled ammo in war now hogs my lazy boy chair
Bigfoot lives here
From battlefield grit to fur in the air
Bigfoot lives here tail like a flag
Sits like a king in a Mustang rag
From bombs to blankets grit to grace
He swats grenades for a five-place
He's a flashburger lean on your cuddle pack pro
Wears fluff like armor wherever he goes
If royalty barked and had no fear
Bigfoot lives here
Yeah Bigfoot lives here
Bigfoot Ranch that's the name you hear
Where legends are born and puppies appear
Raised from the ashes bred for the brave
With hearts so strong they'll ride anyway
You're not just getting a pup you're holding a piece of history
Four stood tall when the world went dark
Now their legacy lives in every bark
They lean they splash they nap and snore
But make no mistake they were made for war
He blocks the door like a velvet wall
Then struts through the kitchen like he owns it all
Steals your couch your bed your snacks
Then leans in close and never looks back
He's not just a dog he's a legend in your home
From Europe to Texas where the lion dogs roam
Snorts through fireworks never flinched
Carries calm the world can’t pinch
They don’t just cuddle they command
They don’t just play they understand
When you welcome one in it’s more than chance
You join the bloodline of giants
You don't just get a pup you hold a piece of history
You don't just get a dog you welcome a legend into your home
You don't just find a friend you join the bloodline of giants
Let the world spin
Let the skeptics sneer
They've never met the giants
Bigfoot lives here

songs.title: Bloodline Survives
songs.description: Generational strength doesn't skip. It bends, it breaks, it gets buried — but it always comes back. This song is about the women in my family who survived everything so I could be here writing songs about surviving everything.
songs.seo_description: Generational strength that bends but never breaks. Bloodline Survives is an original country rock anthem by The Dogmother about the women who survived everything so the next generation could thrive.

A 170-pound Leonberger doing the wiggle wiggle doggy dance with zero shame. So I made it into a club track. You're welcome.

songs.title: Breathe
songs.description: Sometimes the only thing standing between you and a breakdown is one slow breath. I wrote this during a season when everything was loud — the grief, the anger, the noise in my own head. I needed something that would just... let me land. This is that landing. Put it on, close your eyes, and come back to yourself.
songs.seo_description: When the world gets too loud and you need to come back to yourself. Breathe is an original healing frequency by The Dogmother -- close your eyes, let go, and let the music bring you home.

songs.title: Christmas on the Beach
songs.description: Not everyone does Christmas in the snow. Some of us do it with sand between our toes, salt in our hair, and a sunset that looks like God wrapped it in ribbon. This is Christmas, Texas coast style.
songs.seo_description: Santa in flip-flops, palm trees with tinsel, and a steel drum Christmas. Christmas on the Beach is original holiday trop rock by The Dogmother -- Christmas, Texas coast style, with sand between your toes.
songs.lyrics:
Valentina

Christmas on the beach and I'm feeling alright,
Santa's in flip-flops, what a jolly sight.
Palm trees sparkling with tinsel and light,
Seashell stars shining in the tropical night.

No frosty windows, no icy roads,
We're stringing up lights where the ocean breeze blows.
Santa's riding jet skis, sleigh's on a boat,
Heat waves rising while carolers float.

Kids building sandmen with buckets and pails,
Seashells for noses, sunglasses for eyes.
Santa works his tan with reindeer shades,
Beach balls bouncing while the ukulele plays.

While the world shovels snow, I've got sand at my feet,
A seashell star glowing where the palm trees meet.
Heat waves caroling with a steel drum beat,
Merry Christmas in the tropical heat.

songs.title: Christmas Puppy
songs.description: Pure joy wrapped in fur. This song is about the moment a puppy shows up under the Christmas tree and nothing else matters. The world stops. The kids scream. The dog has no idea what's happening. And everything is perfect.
songs.seo_description: That moment a puppy shows up under the tree and nothing else in the world matters. Christmas Puppy is an original holiday song by The Dogmother about the best gift that ever wagged its tail.
songs.lyrics:
Valentina

Puppy for Christmas, waiting under the tree,
Santa bring me a puppy, tie a bow just for me.
Stockings full of biscuits, jingles in the air,
Best gift ever, Santa — love is everywhere.

Forget the shiny gadgets, forget the diamond rings,
Joy is in the barking when the little furball sings.
Collars jingling brightly, tails that wag all day,
Puppy kisses make it the merriest holiday.

Snowflakes on the window, paw prints in the snow,
Little tails are wagging, hearts are full of cheer,
Puppy love's the magic gift that lasts the whole year.

Puppy love, puppy cheer,
Puppy magic every year.
Wrap them up in love,
That's the gift that always grows.

songs.title: Coffee Constellation
songs.description: Written at 4 AM staring at the coffee swirling in the cup and seeing the universe in it. When you can't sleep and the night feels infinite, even a cup of coffee becomes a constellation. I think the best songs come from insomnia — when the world goes quiet and the only thing louder than your thoughts is the drip of the coffee maker.

songs.title: Daddy's Eyes
songs.description:
JT wrote this one — and he could barely get through recording it.

This song is about a father who had his child ripped away from him through parental alienation. Not because he was dangerous. Not because he didn't love her. Because someone decided to weaponize a child against the man who used to mow the yard with her strapped to his back like a butterfly.

She filed the papers. Blocked the calls. Burned the photos. Told the child daddy was gone. But blood doesn't lie. And somewhere out there, that little girl still has her daddy's eyes — the one thing they couldn't erase.

If you've ever had your child taken from you by the system, by lies, by someone who turned love into a weapon — this song is yours. You're not forgotten. And neither is your child.
songs.seo_description: A father's love that doesn't need words because it lives in every look and every sleepless night. Daddy's Eyes is an original country ballad by The Dogmother about the love between a parent and child that nothing can erase.

songs.title: Ghost Wanted Dead or Alive
songs.description:
They didn't just try to ruin him.

They built a whole damn mission around it.

Watching.
Tracking.
Collecting pieces like if they just had enough—
*this time* it would finally stick.

Every rumor got bigger.
Every version got darker.

*He's the devil.*
*He ran.*
*He's hiding.*
*He's done.*

But here's the part that keeps breaking their script—

He never fell.

Not once.

And that's what turned it into obsession.

Because when you throw everything you've got at someone—
lies, pressure, control, narrative—

and they still walk out of it standing?

You don't let that go.

You chase it.

You try to explain it.
You try to fix the ending so it makes sense again.

But it won't.

Because the truth doesn't match the story they told.

The "devil" they tried to create?

He didn't burn.
He built a life.
Moved on. Thriving. Untouched in all the ways that were supposed to break him.

And the more they watch—
the worse it gets.

Because now every rumor they started just circles back to one thing:

Proof they were wrong.
Proof they never had control.
Proof they never will.

So they keep talking.
Keep chasing.
Keep trying to find something that isn't there.

And the whole time—
he doesn't answer. doesn't explain. doesn't even look back.

He just lives.

And that's what makes him the ghost.

Not because he disappeared.

Because no matter how hard they try—
they can't stop watching him… and they can't get him out of their head.
songs.seo_description: They threw everything at him -- lies, pressure, control -- and he still walked out standing. Ghost Wanted Dead or Alive is original outlaw music by The Dogmother about becoming untouchable when they wanted you destroyed.

songs.title: Goodnight Baby
songs.description:
If this ever finds you…

I don't know where you are when you're reading this… or what your life feels like right now… but I know you. I know your heart. I know the little girl who used to save me the last cup of pretend tea like it mattered more than anything in the world. I know the way you used to hum down the hallway like the whole house depended on your light. And I still hear that girl. She didn't disappear to me. Not for one second.

I have replayed your life in my head a thousand times… from the moment they laid you on my chest and everything in me went still because my soul recognized yours. You weren't just my daughter. You were my miracle. You were the one God trusted me with.

You are still my baby. I don't care how old you are. I don't care what life looks like now. I don't care how much time has passed. There is nothing — nothing — you could ever do that would make me close my arms to you.

And if you ever need a way out… if you ever feel stuck, scared, or alone… You can come to me. No questions. No judgment. No conditions.

I love you to God's nose and back again. To infinity and beyond times ten. And I am still here… waiting for you.

songs.title: Luminarias

songs.seo_description: Thousands of paper bags with candles lining Canyon Road like a prayer made of light. Luminarias is an original holiday song by The Dogmother about walking through the cold in Santa Fe at Christmas, surrounded by warmth and someone you love.

Candles in the paper bags, soft warm light.
Santa rides the border where the palm trees sway,
Christmas Spanish style on this holy day.

Farolitos line the streets of clay,
Children laugh while the guitars play.
Steel drums echo, wooden flutes reply,
Peace on the mesa, stars fill the sky.

Blankets on shoulders when the night gets cold.

From the pueblos to the sea, hear the voices sing,
Every culture's gift for the newborn King.
Christmas Spanish style — timeless rhyme.

songs.title: One Night with the Devil
songs.description: The preacher's wife and the devil walked into a small town — and the whole place lost its mind. Rumors rolled through Sunday pews. Hymns in her throat but the whispers grew louder. She wasn't supposed to want him. He wasn't supposed to exist in her world. But one night changed everything — and small-town gossip lit a wildfire that burned so beautiful nobody could look away. This is that forbidden love story. The kind the church won't talk about but everybody lives.
songs.seo_description: Dark, dangerous, and one night that changes everything. One Night with the Devil is original country rock by The Dogmother -- the song that plays in the part of the movie where she makes the wrong choice and the right story.

songs.title: Sleep
songs.description:
After everything — the court battles, the gaslighting, the nights I couldn't stop my brain from replaying every fight — I couldn't sleep. For months. My body was wrecked, my nervous system was shot, and every night was just me staring at the ceiling reliving the worst moments of my life.

So I studied it. I dove into the science behind healing frequencies, how certain sounds actually calm the nervous system, how specific tones tell your brain it's safe to let go. I learned about binaural beats, solfeggio frequencies, the way sound physically changes your heart rate and brainwave patterns.

Then I wrote this song. Not for streaming numbers. Not for a playlist. I wrote it because I needed to sleep. I needed my body to stop fighting. I needed permission to rest.

If you're lying awake right now with a brain that won't shut off — this is for you. Close your eyes. Let it in. You're safe here.
songs.seo_description: Permission to rest. No lyrics, no demands -- just the sound of being gently taken down. Sleep is an original healing frequency by The Dogmother designed for insomnia, anxiety, and the nights that won't end.

songs.title: This is Shame
songs.description:
I wrote this song the year my brother died — while his ashes sat on my studio desk. I stared at that box every single day, and it changed me forever.

My brother wasn't "crazy." He wasn't "the problem." He was a human being in pain — and the world slapped labels on him instead of offering help. People gaslit him, dismissed him, mocked him, silenced him. And when he finally broke, they blamed HIM for the pain they helped cause.

That's why I wrote this song.

Mental health matters. Empathy matters. And for the love of God — stop gaslighting people and calling them crazy for the pain you caused.

This song is for every person who was misunderstood, mislabeled, and emotionally abandoned. It's for the ones who were hurting and got told to "get over it." It's for the ones who were silenced until the silence swallowed them.

I sing now because he never got to. And I will never let another person shame me, mute me, or call me "crazy" for telling the truth.
songs.seo_description: The song that started it all -- the one she couldn't get through without crying. Shame isn't what they did to you. It's what they made you carry. This is Shame is original music by The Dogmother about putting it down.

This song is about that ex who never moved on — the one who can't stop watching your life even though they lost the right to be in it. You left. You healed. You grew. And they're still obsessed.
songs.seo_description: Years later and they're still scrolling your page at 2 AM. Still stalking. Still gossiping like it's their full-time job. Obsessed is original outlaw country by The Dogmother for the ex who can't stop watching your life.
songs.lyrics:
Still Obsessed

Still watching my life, scrolling the scene, Lost in the shadow of what might have been. She hears my name when you talk at night, But obsession don’t fade — it don’t feel right.

You swore I broke you, said you’d never believe, But the truth is simple — you can’t leave. Years go by, you call her your life, But you won’t call her your wife.

Still obsessed, still obsessed, My name’s the ghost you can’t forget Still obsessed, still obsessed, You can’t replace me — forever set..

You spit your venom, but it fed my flame, I rose from the ashes, you stayed the same. You smear my name, but it only proves, Every word you breathe comes back to you.

I don’t defend, I don’t give in, Freedom’s my fire, carved from within. You curse my name, but you can’t destroy, The echo you hear is the sound of my joy.

Still obsessed, still obsessed, My name’s the ghost you can’t forget Still obsessed, still obsessed, You can’t replace me — forever set..

(whispered, rising into chant) I’m the ghost in your story, the line you repeat, The name on your tongue, the ache in defeat. It drives you insane, but I remain, Your curse, your shadow — haunted by my name.

Big paws flipping snow on their nose,
By the fire’s glow, the love only grows.

Dog bones in stockings, toys under the tree,
Saving turkey scraps for the dogs and me.
I’ll find you under the mistletoe

Slobbery kiss under the mistletoe (slobbery kiss, slobbery kiss…)
Paw prints shining in the falling snow (falling snow, falling snow…)
Gentle giants in the firelight’s glow,
It’s a Bigfoot Christmas — love’s what we show.

It’s a Bigfoot night and the bonfire’s bright,
Dogs chase sparks through the winter night.
From Leonberger pride to the barn cat’s purr,
Every gift is wrapped in Christmas fur.

Dog bones in stockings, toys under the tree,
Saving turkey scraps for the dogs and me
I’ll find you under the mistletoe

Slobbery kiss under the mistletoe (slobbery kiss, slobbery kiss…)
Paw prints shining in the falling snow (falling snow, falling snow…)
Gentle giants in the firelight’s glow,
It’s a Bigfoot Christmas — love’s what we show.

Tails thump louder than the Christmas band,
Joy this big you can’t hold in your hands.
Every woof and howl is a carol we know,
Love wrapped in fur, hearts all aglow.
I’ll find you under the mistletoe

Slobbery kiss under the mistletoe (slobbery kiss, slobbery kiss…)
Paw prints shining in the falling snow (falling snow, falling snow…)
Gentle giants in the firelight’s glow,
It’s a Bigfoot Christmas — love’s what we show.

songs.description: Different from Tattoo in Albuquerque. Same ink, different story. Every tattoo is a chapter. This one's the whole book.
songs.seo_description: Every tattoo is a chapter. This one's the whole book. Tattoo is original country soul by The Dogmother -- different story from Tattoo In Albuquerque, same ink, same survival, same refusal to be erased.

Dusty roads sacred stone
Every scar says you're not alone
Blood and fire sky so blue
Tattoo in Albuquerque forever burning through

Tatto and ink
The needle burned the silence fire cut my skin
The desert in the colors and the mirror grinned
Not just paint or shadow not just flesh and bone
The desert carved its memory it marked me as its own

Ancient ghosts were whispering shadows pulled me near
Desert winds kept telling me your missing truth is here
Every road that broke me every star that pulled me through
The desert runs inside me my forever truth

Dusty roads sacred stone
Every scar says you're not alone
Blood and fire sky so blue
Tattoo in Albuquerque forever burning through

songs.title: Luminaries
songs.description: Not the Christmas luminarias — the people. The ones who light the way. The ones who glow so others can see. This song is for them.
songs.seo_description: Not the Christmas luminarias -- the people. The ones who light the way so others can see. Luminaries is original southwest country soul by The Dogmother about the humans who glow in the darkness.
songs.lyrics:
Luminarias glowing in the desert night,
Candles in the paper bags, soft warm light.
Santa rides the border where the palm trees sway,
Christmas Spanish style — on this holy day.

Farolitos line the streets of clay,
Children laugh while the guitars play.
Steel drums echo, wooden flutes reply,
Peace on the mesa, stars fill the sky.

Luminarias glowing in the desert night,
Candles in the paper bags, soft warm light.
Santa rides the border where the palm trees sway,
Christmas Spanish style — on this holy day.

Tamales on the table, stories retold,
Blankets on the shoulders when the night gets cold.
Drums keep the rhythm, the reggae beats roll,
A fusion of the spirits making one soul.

From the pueblos to the sea, hear the voices sing,
Every culture's gift for the newborn King.
Wood flutes whisper, steel drums chime,
Christmas Spanish style — timeless rhyme.

Luminarias glowing in the desert night,
Candles in the paper bags, soft warm light.
Santa rides the border where the palm trees sway,
Christmas Spanish style — on this holy day.

lyrics.full_text:
Fields of Gold
Valentina

Dust on the highway, storm fading fast,
Seasons of sorrow, but they don't last.
Chased greener pastures, chased rainbows untold,
I found my treasure in fields of gold.

Fields of gold, fields of gold,
This is the story my heart has told.
Fields of gold, fields of gold,
I found my home, I found my home.

Wildflowers blazing, horizon wide,
Every hard mile now justified.
The end of searching, the end of cold,
I found forever in fields of gold.

Fields of gold, fields of gold,
Blessings and promise in every fold.
Fields of gold, fields of gold,
Love is the fortune I get to hold.

No greener pastures, no brighter road,
I was lost till your light showed.
Every rainbow, every sign foretold,
Led me here to fields of gold.

Fields of gold, fields of gold,
This is the story my soul has told.
Fields of gold, fields of gold,
I found my forever, I found my home.

Las Cruces Nights
Valentina

Las Cruces nights,
Galaxy high,
Land of enchantment,
You and I.

Chocolate eddies on your tongue, feel the world unwind,
Heat waves shimmer, but your hand's in mine.
Organ Mountains glow like fire in the haze,
We got lost in forever, in that desert maze.

Wrapped in a blanket, the coyotes sing,
Every laugh was a promise, every touch a flame.
Cold night air, but your body was warm,
Two souls electric in a desert storm.

Ojos de luna, corazon de sol,
The desert keeps secrets it will never let go.
Every star was a vow, every breath was a prayer,
Las Cruces, mi amor, I'll always meet you there.

In the land of enchantment where the desert breathes light,
Heat in the daytime, cold stars at night.
Hearts slow-dancing on a galaxy high,
We'll always have those Las Cruces nights.

Rainbow
Valentina

Thunder rolling, skies so gray,
Lightning chasing hope away.
You're searching high, you're searching low,
But I'm the light you'll never know.

I am the rainbow — when your sky turns black,
I am the rainbow — you can't chase me back.
I am the rainbow — at the end of your road,
I am the rainbow — the story you were told.

Storm clouds break, the shadows fall,
Through the silence, hear my call.
No gold to find, no pot to claim,
I'm the fire inside your rain.

Don't look up, don't look down,
I'm the gold you never found.
No illusion, no painted show,
I am the rainbow.

I am the rainbow — when your sky turns black,
I am the rainbow — you can't chase me back.
I am the rainbow — at the end of your road,
I am the rainbow — the truth you always know.

Still The Asshole
Valentina

Years later, you're still cryin',
Wearin' your crown but the world ain't buyin'.
Fake your scars for a hometown show,
But I was there — and the truth still knows.

You're still the asshole, after all this time,
Wearin' your crown, but the truth is mine.
You're still the asshole, can't rewrite the night,
I walked out free, and I walk in light.
Still the asshole, still the asshole,
Still the asshole, still the asshole.
I walk in light, I walk in light,
I walk in light, I walk in light.

Flying monkeys cheer your name,
But behind closed doors it's a different game.
Stories twisted, day versus night,
The one who walks free is the one in light.

Fake victim crown, hold it high,
But the crowd can see the lie.
Truth don't bend, it don't fade —
Freedom's the fire I already made.

Tattoo In Albuquerque
Valentina

Dusty roads, sacred stone,
Every scar says you're not alone.
Blood and fire, sky so blue,
Tattoo in Albuquerque — burning through.

The needle burned the silence, fire cut my skin,
The desert in the colors and the mirror grinned.
Not just paint or shadow, not just flesh and bone,
The desert carved its memory — it marked me as its own.

Ancient ghosts were whisperin', shadows pulled me near,
Desert winds kept tellin' me, your missing truth is here.
Every road that broke me, every star that pulled me through,
The desert runs inside me — my forever truth.

Puff Planet
Valentina

Puff Planet — where the sky stay hazy,
Devil's lettuce got the desert all lazy.
Should I get a job? Maybe… maybe…
I'll just sit here gettin' stoned, go crazy.

Puff Planet — I'm baked, I'm fried,
Puff Planet — Uber denied,
Puff Planet — brain's outside,
Puff Planet — take another ride.

Green chile smoke? Nah, that's just haze,
Santa Fe streets lookin' spaced for days.
The plug pulled up with a sack so fat,
Baked chicken in my head, can't drive like that.
Clouds roll deep on Stoner Road,
Lit-up signs say "high" wherever you go.
Sky high tourists, spaced out crew,
Even Roswell aliens got the munchies too.

Said I'd call my boss… forgot his name,
Forgot my password, forgot my game.
Rolling papers thicker than the Bible verse,
If Snoop hears this track, he might reimburse.
Faded smoke rings, float like balloons,
Carlsbad Caverns hotboxed by noon.
Even the coyotes howlin' lit tonight,
Welcome to Puff Planet, we spaced all right.

Las Cruces lunch, chile cheese fries,
Taos tacos stacked a mile high.
Albuquerque gummies, chocolate eddies more,
Even Grandma's stoned, raiding the store.
Santa Fe brownies, bless her soul,
She's laughin' at cartoons, losin' control.
From churros to sopapillas, life tastes divine,
Grandma said, "Pass that joint — it's mine!"

Ashtray preacher, Sunday choir,
Pass that joint a little higher.
Work? Responsibilities? What for?
I'm puffin' promotions at the corner store.

Christmas on the Beach
Valentina

Christmas on the beach and I'm feeling alright,
Santa's in flip-flops, what a jolly sight.
Palm trees sparkling with tinsel and light,
Seashell stars shining in the tropical night.

No frosty windows, no icy roads,
We're stringing up lights where the ocean breeze blows.
Santa's riding jet skis, sleigh's on a boat,
Heat waves rising while carolers float.

Kids building sandmen with buckets and pails,
Seashells for noses, sunglasses for eyes.
Santa works his tan with reindeer shades,
Beach balls bouncing while the ukulele plays.

While the world shovels snow, I've got sand at my feet,
A seashell star glowing where the palm trees meet.
Heat waves caroling with a steel drum beat,
Merry Christmas in the tropical heat.

Don't Cry At My Funeral
Valentina

Save your tears, save your lies,
You weren't there in my life.
I bled alone, I broke in two,
Now you want a front-row view.

Don't cry at my funeral,
Keep your fake love out of my life.

You should've fought while I still breathed,
Not turned your back, not ghosted, not leave.
Tears come cheap when I'm no more,
Too late for love you never fought for.

Don't pretend you're sad,
Just talk your trash like you always had.
Don't change your tune 'cause I'm stone cold dead,
Be who you were — show your colors instead.

I curse your mask, I strip your disguise,
The truth will haunt you, it never dies.
Justice is patient, revenge is slow,
Karma's the shadow that won't let you go.

Don't cry at my funeral,
Keep your fake love out of my life.
Your goodbye was long before I died.
Karma's watching.

Favorite Mistake
Valentina

You never wrote me love songs, not a line.
You never brought me flowers, not one time.
I can't stand your voice when you call my name.
I hate your face when you play that game.

Too young, too proud, too blind to see,
We built a fire, burned down the tree.

You're my favorite mistake of mine,
And I'd do it all over, every time.
The shame, the fights, the grace, the ache,
The family remains — my favorite mistake.

I think you're a bitch, I won't pretend.
And you're still an asshole, till the bitter end.
We broke each other, burned down love,
But the roots still rise — and that's enough.

Too old to blame, too tired to fight,
The family we built is the proof of life.

We hated, we loved, we broke, we bled.
But the family stands when we're both dead.

Favorite mistake — every time.
Favorite mistake — still mine.
Too old to blame, too proud to bend,
We broke it, we built it, we'd do it again.
You're my favorite mistake of mine.

Fireflies & Fireworks
Valentina

Whispers on the porch swing, no one else was there,
You traced the constellations, July in your stare.
I memorized the silence, the way the night was turning,
Holding on to nothing — but God, we were burning.

But you can't hold the sky, can't bottle the flame,
The brightest things vanish when you call them by name.

Fireflies and fireworks, fading in the dark,
Fireflies and fireworks, still burning in my heart.
Summer nights, July skies, you and me — a spark,
Fireflies and fireworks, love's eternal mark.

Moonlight on your shoulders, smoke rising slow,
We carved our names in shadows only we would know.
The world could not contain us, we broke it with our yearning,
Forever lived a moment — and still, we were burning.

One night can outlive a thousand years,
One spark can survive through the smoke and the tears.
You can cage the body, but the memory's free,
Fireflies and fireworks — forever in me.

Lump of Coal
Valentina

Fa-la-la-la-la, you deserve a lump of coal.
Fa-la-la-la-la, you deserve a lump of coal.
Deck the halls with all your drama, bless your bitter soul.
Silent night — only 'cause you ghosted me.
Holy night — sweet hypocrisy.
Fa-la-la-la-la, you deserve a lump of coal.

Please pass the turkey, then pass the blame,
You brag like a carol, it's always the same.
Joy to the world… when you walk out the door.
You've been naughty all year — and Santa keeps score.

Grandma didn't get run over by a reindeer, no —
She hung the stockings, she carried the load.
She hit the nog hard, rolled her eyes and prayed,
Sweet baby Jesus, bless his heart.

O come all ye fakers, faithful to none.
The choir is tired, your solo is done.
Santa's list is short, and your name's in bold.
No gifts, no grace — just a lump of coal.

Villain
Valentina

I'm okay being the villain in your story,
You're the loser in mine, loser in mine.

You need an audience, you need a stage,
Spin your story, set me on the page.
You keep scrolling, sneering, spreading my name,
But your circus burned out — I wrote the last frame.

You chase the noise, I chase the light,
You play for clout and drama every night.
You call me villain, I wear it fine,
The only crown is living my life.

I'm okay being the villain in your story,
You're the loser in mine, loser in mine.
I don't need your crown, don't need your glory,
I'm living just fine, living just fine.

Obsessed with control, can't let me be,
You type the rumors while I'm running free.
You love the chaos, you love the show,
But only hollow voices follow you though.

Call me wicked, call me cold,
Call me every lie you've sold.
You made me the villain so you'd feel big,
But darling, you're the one with the empty throne.

Luminarias
Valentina

Luminarias glowing in the desert night,
Candles in the paper bags, soft warm light.
Santa rides the border where the palm trees sway,
Christmas Spanish style on this holy day.

Farolitos line the streets of clay,
Children laugh while the guitars play.
Steel drums echo, wooden flutes reply,
Peace on the mesa, stars fill the sky.

Tamales on the table, stories retold,
Blankets on shoulders when the night gets cold.
Drums keep the rhythm, the reggae beats roll,
A fusion of spirits making one soul.

From the pueblos to the sea, hear the voices sing,
Every culture's gift for the newborn King.
Wood flutes whisper, steel drums chime,
Christmas Spanish style — timeless rhyme.

Choke On The Wine
Valentina

Don't mistake my silence for weakness — no.
It's not my rage you should fear — it's my restraint.
It's the silence that should haunt you, the quiet you mocked.
Judgment's coming — and I'm the one who knocks.

You crowned yourself martyr — but the metal is tin.
Your circus sells lies; my blessings cash in.
I built in silence what you tried to burn.
Blessings speak louder than alibis earned.

My name is the ghost on your tongue you can't shake,
My name is the ghost on your tongue you can't shake.

The mirror cracks — your crooked grin.
Reflections argue who let you in.
The clock is drowning, the seconds spin.
Time's a thief with your bottle of sin.

Every drop screams my name.
Every drip feeds the flame.

Feast of judgment — I'm the host.
Take your seat, it's truth you toast.
Heaven keeps the ledger, the scars, the names.
Evidence sings when I don't — it testifies your shame.

Choke on the wine, choke on the wine,
You buried me deep, thought I'd never return.
I crawled from the grave, I walked through the burn.
How long did you think I would swallow the lie?
Now judgment has come — and you choke on the wine.

Stains on the table — stains on your hands.
History written in crimson bands.
Choke on the wine, choke on the wine.
My life is the light that sings, revealing the truth of who you are.

Christmas Puppy
Valentina

Puppy for Christmas, waiting under the tree,
Santa bring me a puppy, tie a bow just for me.
Stockings full of biscuits, jingles in the air,
Best gift ever, Santa — love is everywhere.

Forget the shiny gadgets, forget the diamond rings,
Joy is in the barking when the little furball sings.
Collars jingling brightly, tails that wag all day,
Puppy kisses make it the merriest holiday.

Snowflakes on the window, paw prints in the snow,
Little tails are wagging, hearts are full of cheer,
Puppy love's the magic gift that lasts the whole year.

Puppy love, puppy cheer,
Puppy magic every year.
Wrap them up in love,
That's the gift that always grows.

Enchanted Green Haze
Valentina

Enchanted haze over New Mexico skies,
Green haze in your eyes.
Enchanted haze, Las Cruces nights so high,
Green haze tonight.

Sunset burns on the Organ range,
Colors shifting, everything feels strange.
Whispers ride on the desert breeze,
Your hand in mine makes the moment freeze.

Breathe in slow — let the city fade away,
Hold me close — hear the mountains say.

From the bluffs the skyline starts to glow,
City lights like diamonds scattered below.
Mesmerized, drifting, lost in your gaze,
Hearts caught up in the green night's haze.

Time stands still, we're weightless, we fly,
Wrapped in the glow of the desert sky.
Lost in the moment, nothing remains,
But love and the fire of this green haze.

It's a Bigfoot Christmas
Valentina

Slobbery kiss under the mistletoe,
Paw prints shining in the falling snow,
Gentle giants in the firelight's glow,
It's a Bigfoot Christmas — love's what we show.

Ornaments fall from the Christmas tree,
Tails wag wild in their holiday glee.
Big paws flipping snow on their nose,
By the fire's glow, the love only grows.

Dog bones in stockings, toys under the tree,
Saving turkey scraps for the dogs and me.
I'll find you under the mistletoe.

It's a Bigfoot night and the bonfire's bright,
Dogs chase sparks through the winter night.
From Leonberger pride to the barn cat's purr,
Every gift is wrapped in Christmas fur.

Tails thump louder than the Christmas band,
Joy this big you can't hold in your hands.
Every woof and howl is a carol we know,
Love wrapped in fur, hearts all aglow.
I'll find you under the mistletoe.

Still Obsessed
Valentina

Still watching my life, scrolling the scene,
Lost in the shadow of what might have been.
She hears my name when you talk at night,
But obsession don't fade — it don't feel right.

You swore I broke you, said you'd never believe,
But the truth is simple — you can't leave.
Years go by, you call her your life,
But you won't call her your wife.

Still obsessed, still obsessed,
My name's the ghost you can't forget.
Still obsessed, still obsessed,
You can't replace me — forever set.

You spit your venom, but it fed my flame,
I rose from the ashes, you stayed the same.
You smear my name, but it only proves,
Every word you breathe comes back to you.

I don't defend, I don't give in,
Freedom's my fire, carved from within.
You curse my name, but you can't destroy,
The echo you hear is the sound of my joy.

Still obsessed, still obsessed,
My name's the ghost you can't forget.
Still obsessed, still obsessed,
You can't replace me — forever set.
